name: Wild Game Pate
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Lean pork and 3/4 lb lean Veal, finely ground together
recipeInstructions:
First, marinate the strips of game. In a non-reactive bowl, mix together the marinade ingredients: thyme, salt, pepper, allspice, and green onions. Add the game strips and marinate while preparing the stuffing.
For stuffing, cook onions slowly in butter until tender. Scrape into a large mixing bowl. Pour cognac into the same skillet and boil until reduced by half. Scrape the pan drippings into the bowl with the onions. Add pork and veal, beaten eggs, salt, pepper, allspice, thyme, and garlic. Beat vigorously with a wooden spoon until thoroughly blended.
To assemble the pate, line the bottom and sides of a loaf pan with blanched bacon (blanch by plunging into boiling water, removing immediately, and draining briefly). Reserve some strips for the top. Drain game strips, reserving marinade. Beat marinade into stuffing. Divide stuffing into three parts. Arrange one third in the bottom of the loaf pan, cover with half the game strips, repeat layers with another third of stuffing and remaining half of game strips, spread the remaining stuffing on top, place a bay leaf on top, and cover with the remaining blanched bacon.
Cover the pan with aluminum foil and set in a larger pan of boiling water (water should come about halfway up the outside of the loaf pan). Bake in an oven preheated to 350°F for 1.5 hours. Remove the loaf pan from the water and place another, preferably glass, loaf pan on top of the foil-wrapped pate. Cool at room temperature several hours or overnight. Chill, still weighted down. Serve in slices with crackers.
NOTE: A "pinch" is about 1/8 teaspoon.
